title: Multi-script harmonization: Latin + community script in one unified family
context: Multi-script harmonization — combining Latin and an under-served script in one type family with consistent weight, spacing, and visual style — is advanced type design work. It requires both the Latin family (fonts-ship-104) and a community-co-designed script (fonts-script-204) to be stable, and needs co-design with both communities. This is a future/backlog item with significant complexity and community coordination requirements.
objective: Design and draw a harmonized multi-script family that combines the Latin accessibility family and at least one community co-designed script in a single, visually consistent type family with shared weight, spacing, and stylistic rationale.
acceptanceCriteria: Both the Latin family and the target script family are stable and released before this work begins.
Both affected communities (Latin accessibility reviewers and the script community) have co-signed the harmonization brief.
Unified family maintains visual harmony: consistent weight contrast, x-height relationship, and spacing rhythm between scripts.
All script-specific OpenType GSUB/GPOS features are preserved and verified clean via HarfBuzz.
FontBakery Universal + GF profiles pass (no FAIL) for the unified family.
provenance.json covers all glyphs in both scripts; no proprietary derivation.
output: A pull request adding a harmonized multi-script font family (Latin + community script) with shared UFO source structure, passing CI gates, and provenance documentation for both script communities.
